data.title: SIERRA BANCORP 1Q 2026: Net income $12.5M, EPS $0.96— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
SIERRA BANCORP reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $12.5M, up from $9.1M in the year-ago quarter, driven by higher noninterest income and lower credit loss provisions while revenue was not separately reported in Part I Items 1–2.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income was $12.5M for 1Q 2026, compared with $9.1M in 1Q 2025; YoY change 37.4%.
  • Diluted EPS was $0.96 for 1Q 2026, compared with $0.65 in 1Q 2025; YoY change 47.7%.
  • Revenue: Not reported for the quarter in Part I Items 1–2 and therefore omitted.

Business Highlights

  • Net income growth: Earnings rose 38% year over year to $12.5M, supported by increased noninterest income and lower provisions for credit losses.
  • Revenue mix and margins: Net interest income increased modestly; lower funding costs offset slightly lower asset yields and NIM remained about 3.75%.
  • Deposits and funding: Deposits increased 2%, with core noninterest-bearing balances improving the funding mix and liquidity.
  • Loan activity: Gross loans declined roughly 3% due to seasonal mortgage warehouse paydowns and lower line utilization; focus remains on local community and CRE lending.
  • Efficiency and income drivers: Noninterest income rose about 20% (service fees, BOLI, FHLB dividends) and expenses fell through lower deferred compensation and cost controls.

Original SEC Filing: SIERRA BANCORP [ BSRR ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Nabors Industries 1Q 2026: Revenue $783.55M, EPS ($1.54) — 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Nabors Industries reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue up year-over-year but a swing to a net loss and negative EPS compared with the prior-year quarter, reflecting higher activity mixed with margin pressures.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$783.55M$736.19M6.4%
Net income²($15.17M)$32.99M(146%)
Diluted EPS³($1.54)$2.18(170.6%)

¹ Reported as “Operating revenues”. ² Reported as “Net income (loss) attributable to Nabors”. ³ Reported as “Earnings (losses) per share”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue rose to $783.5M in Q1 2026, up $47.4M YoY driven by higher activity across segments.
  • International Drilling led growth with a 10% revenue increase and rigs working up 9%, outperforming U.S. growth.
  • The Parker acquisition (closed Mar. 11, 2025) added a full quarter of Drilling Solutions revenue and expanded Nabors' global footprint.
  • Average rigs working increased 10% in the U.S. and 9% internationally, though U.S. dayrate pressure offset some revenue gains.
  • Strong operating cash flow supported $165M of quarterly capital expenditures for rig enhancements and new equipment.

Original SEC Filing: NABORS INDUSTRIES LTD [ NBR ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

data.title: HF Sinclair Corp 1Q 2026: Revenue $7.12B, EPS $3.56— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
HF Sinclair Corp reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $7.12B and diluted EPS of $3.56, driven by higher product prices and volumes versus the prior-year quarter.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$7.12B$6.37B11.8%
Net income²$648M($4M)16300%
Diluted EPS³$3.56($0.02)17900%

¹ Reported as “Sales and other revenues (Note 3)”. ² Reported as “Net income (loss) attributable to HF Sinclair stockholders”. ³ Reported as “Earnings (loss) per share attributable to HF Sinclair stockholders”.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth: Sales rose about 12% year-over-year to $7.1B, reflecting higher prices and volumes.
  • Refining performance: Adjusted refinery gross margin per barrel increased 9% to $9.95, led by strength in the West region.
  • Renewables momentum: Renewables volumes and margins improved due to BOHO spread narrowing, higher RINs and a $49M prior-year PTC benefit.
  • Marketing & brand: Sinclair branded sites grew roughly 6% to 1,769 locations, supporting sales and margins.
  • Operational milestones: Completed turnarounds at Puget Sound and Woods Cross and added the Industrial Oils Unlimited acquisition; Q2 run-rate refinery throughput guidance is 600k–630k BPD.

Original SEC Filing: HF Sinclair Corp [ DINO ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

data.title: Glen Burnie Bancorp Q1 profit falls
data.description:
Overview

  • US community bank's Q1 net income returned to profit after prior quarter loss; falls y-o-y

  • Q1 net interest income rose q-o-q and y-o-y

  • Loans and deposits grew in Q1 vs prior qtr, while noninterest expense declined

Outlook

  • Company intends to focus on disciplined loan growth and prudent liquidity management

  • Company expects Annapolis market expansion to support commercial lending and deposit growth

  • Company may use wholesale funding to support loan growth and manage liquidity

Result Drivers

  • ONE-TIME LOAN INTEREST - Q1 net interest income and margin benefited from $167,000 in one-time loan interest income, including a positive adjustment on a purchased loan pool and interest collected on a repaid nonaccrual loan

  • LOAN AND DEPOSIT GROWTH - Loan growth compared with prior qtr was primarily from purchased consumer and commercial loans, while deposits increased across several categories including noninterest-bearing and brokered deposits

  • LOWER NONINTEREST EXPENSE - Noninterest expense declined from the prior quarter, mainly due to lower professional fees and reduced consulting costs following efficiency initiatives

data.title: Oil prices drop as Iran offers new proposal for peace talks with US
data.description:
According to Iranian state news agency IRNA, Iran has sent its latest proposal for talks to Pakistani mediators.

Oil prices fell on the news and by 1550 BST, Brent crude was down 2% at $108.20 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate was 3.7% lower at $101.17.

Pakistani officials reportedly told CBS News on Friday that a revised Iranian response to the latest US terms to end the war had been conveyed to American officials, confirming the report by Iranian state media.

Earlier in the week, Trump rejected an Iranian proposal to first open the Strait of Hormuz and lift the blockade, and postpone nuclear talks to a later stage. Trump told Axios he would keep Iran under a naval blockade until the regime agrees to a deal that addresses US concerns about its nuclear program.

Axios also reported on Thursday that the US Central Command had prepared plans for a "short and powerful" wave of strikes on Iran in hopes of breaking the negotiating deadlock.

data.title: Louisiana-Pacific Files 8K - Other Events >LPX
data.description:
Louisiana-Pacific Corp. (LPX) filed a Form 8K - Other Events - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on May 01, 2026.

On May 1, 2026, Louisiana-Pacific Corporation (the "Company") announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend to common stockholders of $0.30 per share. The dividend will be payable May 28, 2026, to stockholders of record as of May 14, 2026.

The press release issued by the Company in connection with the dividend is included as Exhibit 99.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and incorporated into this Item 8.01 by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/60519/000006051926000014/lpx-20260501.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/60519/000006051926000014/0000060519-26-000014-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Gold turns positive as oil eases on hopes for Iran talks
data.description:
Key points:
  • Brent prices headed for a weekly gain
  • Iran sends proposal for negotiations to mediator Pakistan
  • U.S. dollar down against peers

By Anjana Anil

Gold rose on Friday, reversing earlier losses of more than 1%, on hopes for a breakthrough to end the Iran war after Tehran reportedly submitted a new proposal for negotiations, easing some inflation concerns.

Spot gold was up 0.3% to $4,636.72 per ounce at 10:36 a.m. ET (1436 GMT) after falling to as low as $4,559.48 earlier in the session. It was still on track for a weekly loss of 1.6%.

U.S. gold futures for June delivery were up 0.4% to $4,649.60.

"The potential peace breakthrough, with Iran signalling that it wants to talk again, probably boosted investor risk appetite, pressured the U.S. dollar index and pushed the gold market higher," said Jim Wyckoff, senior analyst at Kitco Metals.

The dollar was down 0.3% against its peers, making greenback-priced bullion cheaper for buyers holding other currencies.

Iran sent its latest proposal for negotiations with the U.S. to Pakistani mediators on Thursday, Iranian state news agency IRNA reported.

Oil prices dropped on the news, though they remained on track for weekly gains, continuing to fuel concerns about a global economic slowdown and surging inflation as fuel prices climb.

Rising costs could prompt central banks to keep interest rates higher for longer, weighing on non-yielding assets such as gold as investors turn to alternatives like Treasury yields.

The U.S. Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged this week and struck a hawkish tone that saw markets abandon expectations for a rate cut this year. (FEDWATCH)

Bullion prices have fallen since the start of the Iran conflict in late February, despite the metal's traditional role as a hedge against geopolitical uncertainty.

Among other precious metals, spot silver prices rose 3.4% to $76.26 per ounce.

"Long-term outlook (for silver) remains supported by a sixth consecutive annual market deficit, shrinking above-ground inventories and firm demand from solar and private investors," wrote Ole Hansen, head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank.

Platinum was up 0.8% at $2,002, and palladium added 0.9% to $1,537.12.

According to Iranian state news agency IRNA, Iran has sent its latest proposal for talks to Pakistani mediators.

Oil prices fell on the news and by 1550 BST, Brent crude was down 2% at $108.20 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate was 3.7% lower at $101.17.

Pakistani officials reportedly told CBS News on Friday that a revised Iranian response to the latest US terms to end the war had been conveyed to American officials, confirming the report by Iranian state media.

Earlier in the week, Trump rejected an Iranian proposal to first open the Strait of Hormuz and lift the blockade, and postpone nuclear talks to a later stage. data.title: Elon Musk's Tesla Pay Package Valued at $158 Billion for 2025 — WSJ
data.description:
By Becky Peterson

Elon Musk's moonshot compensation package at Tesla came in at $158.4 billion for 2025, according to a regulatory filing.

The pay package was part of a stratospheric compensation plan approved by shareholders last year as a way to motivate Tesla's chief executive to spend more time at the electric-vehicle maker. The 10-year deal could be worth $1 trillion if Musk hits every milestone, which includes growing Tesla's market cap to $8.5 trillion.

Musk won't make the full amount reported, and he has to earn what he does get by hitting significant operational and financial milestones over the next decade.

Musk has already forfeited $26 billion, which was tied to a conditional package known as the "2025 interim award." He gave up the interim package in April after he won access to a 2018 pay package that had previously been held up in court.

Musk, who is also CEO of SpaceX, had threatened on social media to leave Tesla if he didn't grow his ownership to 25%, a figure that he said would enable him to control future artificial intelligence developed by the company.

Musk is poised to control 20.3% of the Tesla vote, once he exercises his outstanding options, which he has committed to doing by Aug. 15. In court Tuesday, he said his 2025 ownership in Tesla was around 15%.

Tesla is currently valued more highly than about a dozen other carmakers combined on the premise that it can deliver a future that includes driverless taxis, humanoid robots and groundbreaking advancements in artificial intelligence.

In November, Tesla shareholders approved the landmark compensation package for Musk that could pay out as much as $1 trillion in stock if certain goals are met over the next 10 years.

The goals include growing Tesla to a $8.5 trillion market capitalization — up from $1.2 trillion today — delivering 20 million cars, putting one million robotaxis into service and selling one million humanoid robots.

The filing Thursday also said Musk had 207.5 million Tesla shares that he could use as collateral.

Write to Becky Peterson at becky.peterson@wsj.com

data.title: Exxon Mobil Loses Ground on Q1 Earnings
data.description:
Exxon Mobil Corporation shares was in the minus column Friday. The oil giant announced first-quarter 2026 earnings of $4.2 billion, or $1.00 per share assuming dilution.

Earnings excluding identified items were $4.9 billion, or $1.16 per share. Earnings were $8.8 billion, or $2.09 per share, excluding identified items and unfavorable estimated timing effects that unwind in subsequent periods. Cash flow from operating activities was $8.7 billion, or $13.8 billion excluding margin postings, which primarily fluctuate with the fair value of underlying derivatives.

Shareholder distributions of $9.2 billion included $4.3 billion of dividends and $4.9 billion of share repurchases, consistent with the company's previously announced plans.

Said CEO Darren Woods, "This quarter demonstrated that ExxonMobil is a fundamentally stronger company than it was just a few years ago, built to perform through disruption and across market cycles. Events in the Middle East tested that strength with the safety of our people remaining our top priority. Those events also underscored the importance of reliable, affordable energy products and the value of the capabilities we have built to deliver them.”

"The underlying business delivered strong results, reflecting the benefits of the strategy we have consistently executed since 2018. We have grown advantaged volumes, optimized our operations, reduced structural costs, and strengthened our earnings power. The result is a more resilient, lower-cost business, grounded in advantaged assets, disciplined capital allocation, and execution excellence. That foundation gives us a durable platform to grow earnings, cash flow, and shareholder value through 2030 and beyond."

XOM shares dipped $1.44 to $153.07.

data.title: European Credit Spreads Do Not Reflect Present Risks — Market Talk
data.description: European credit spreads trade at relatively narrow levels and fail to reflect the risks from the Middle East conflict, RBC BlueBay's Mark Dowding says in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index trades at 290bps, down from 363bps at the peak of the U.S.-Iran conflict, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. Euro credit spreads risk widening, Dowding says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Embattled U.S. manufacturers show their mettle, grow for the fourth month in a row despite Iran war
data.description:
By Jeffry Bartash

ISM shows strongest expansion since 2022 - but inflation is a big problem

A vehicle passes a massive data center under construction in California. A surge in demand for artificial intelligence (AI) is fueling a boom in data centers across the country.

American manufacturers grew in April for the fourth month in a row - the longest streak in four years - but an embryonic recovery faces fresh hurdles from rising oil prices and higher inflation tied to the Iran war.

The Institute for Supply Management's survey of manufacturers stayed at a four-year high of 52.7% in April for the second month in a row. Any number above 50% indicates business is expanding.

The rebound in manufacturing has come as a surprise. Businesses began the year in a cautious stance as they groped with the initial Trump tariffs and soft demand from customers.

Yet a Supreme Court ruling striking down the original tariffs and an accelerating boom in artificial intelligence has supplied fresh momentum to an industry that has struggle to grow for the past four years.

The simmering Iran conflict and high prices for oil, however, are likely to keep a lid on how fast manufacturers can expand. The Trump administration has also put into place replacement tariffs and companies have to figure out how to work around them.

The ISM surveys executives every month about how their businesses are doing. Manufacturers employ about 13 million people and still play a vital role in the U.S. economy.

Key details: Production rose in April at a slightly slower pace compared to March.

New orders, a sign of future sales, rose to a three-month high.

Employment contracted for the 31th consecutive month, suggesting manufacturers are unsure whether the rebound will continue.

A measure of prices jumped to a nearly four-year high in a sign of persistent inflation.

Big picture: Manufacturers, likely the U.S. economy itself, have shown surprising resilience in the face of a series of obstacles to growth.

manufacturers and the economy can grow any faster, economists say, there needs to be an

An end to the Iran war, subsiding oil prices and more clarity on the scope of the new Trump tariffs are necessary, however, to enable manufacturers and the broader U.S. economy to grow any faster.

Looking ahead: "Revenues are very strong, however, price increases are similar to a few years ago with the supply-chain crisis," a top executive at a chemicals makers told ISM.

Market reaction: The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA and S&P 500 SPX rose in Friday trading.

-Jeffry Bartash

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Venezuela's oil exports jump to highest since 2018, with more sales to US, India
data.description:
By Marianna Parraga and Mircely Guanipa

Venezuela's oil exports rose 14% to 1.23 million barrels per day in April, the highest in more than seven years, fueled by more sales to the United States, India and Europe, shipping data and documents from state company PDVSA showed on Friday.

The South American country has been draining oil inventories and recovering crude output in recent months following the U.S. capture of President Nicolas Maduro in January, which led to a flagship supply pact between the governments of U.S. President Donald Trump and Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez.

The agreement, coupled with U.S. licenses easing sanctions on the country, has allowed PDVSA's joint-venture partners and trading houses including Vitol and Trafigura to receive cargoes from the state firm for sales to refiners in the U.S., Europe and Asia.

In April, a total of 66 vessels departed from Venezuelan waters, compared with 61 ships that carried 1.08 million bpd of crude and refined products in March, according to the data, based on tanker movements.

The April average is the highest monthly volume since late 2018, before U.S. sanctions were imposed on Venezuela's energy industry.
